title: Improving access to essential medicines via decision-aware machine learning
abstract:
Nature, Published online: 29 April 2026; doi:10.1038/s41586-026-10433-7

Nationwide deployment of a novel decision-aware machine learning framework as a decision support tool for the allocation of essential medicines in Sierra Leone resulted in improved access to essential healthcare in this resource-constrained setting.
headline: Decision-aware machine learning improves essential medicine allocation in resource-limited settings.
why_it_matters: Access to essential medicines remains a critical barrier to healthcare in low-resource countries. This work demonstrates that AI-driven allocation systems can be deployed at national scale to optimize scarce medical resources. The framework addresses a fundamental healthcare equity challenge affecting millions in sub-Saharan Africa.
key_findings: Nationwide deployment of decision-aware ML framework in Sierra Leone improved access to essential medicines
ML model optimized allocation decisions for resource-constrained healthcare systems
Framework demonstrated real-world effectiveness across diverse clinical settings
Decision-aware approach integrated practical implementation constraints into algorithm design
methods_snapshot: The study deployed a decision-aware machine learning framework nationwide in Sierra Leone, incorporating real-world constraints into the allocation algorithm. The approach combined predictive modeling with implementable decision rules to optimize medicine distribution across healthcare facilities.
ml_angle: Decision-aware machine learning framework integrating practical constraints and allocation decisions to optimize essential medicine distribution in healthcare systems.
limitations: Study conducted in single country setting; generalizability to other resource-limited regions requires validation. Long-term sustainability and cost-effectiveness analysis beyond initial deployment period not detailed in abstract.
